Daily-Life Benefits of Interpersonal Affect Improvement: An Ecological Momentary Assessment Study of Depression and Healthy Controls
10th Conference of the Consortium of European Research on Emotion
Exploring the Temporal Dynamics of Motives and Strategies in Daily Interpersonal Affect Worsening
Conference of the International Society for Research on Emotion
Evaluating the correspondence between qualitative and quantitative measures to assess interpersonal emotion regulation strategies
British Psychology Society 2023 Social Psychology Annual Conference
Parent-reported Pain Intensity Predicts Cognitive Flexibility in Paediatric Patients with Sickle Cell Disease
Understanding the Link between Motives and Strategies in Altruistic and Counter-hedonic Interpersonal Affect Worsening

17th Annual Scientific Conference on Sickle Cell and Thalassaemia
MANUSCRIPTS IN PREPARATION AND UNDER REVIEW
A Digital Shoulder to Cry On: Understanding Why Large Language Models Can Be Effective in Extrinsic Interpersonal Emotion Regulation
Challenges and Evolution of Male and Transgender Sex Work in the Digital Era
Supervisor: Dr. Michael Magcamit, Division of Psychology and Mental Health
Conducted a systematic literature review on emotion regulation and cognitive reappraisal in support of ERC and Leverhulme Trust grant applications (80-hour commissioned post, £1,800).
Research Exchange, Sapienza University of Rome, Italy
Investigated prosocial behaviours and informant discrepancy methodologies; funded by the UK Government Turing Scheme (£1,400).
Collaborator: Professor Katja Upadyaya, University of Helsinki
Competitively selected by the European Association of Developmental Psychology; developing an ongoing collaborative manuscript.
Developmental Pathways in Socioeconomic Status (SES) and Depression
developmental origins of social skill deficits in low SES environments
Examined how early life SES shapes brain development, physiological markers, and depression risk
Analyzed developmental trajectories of social functioning across socioeconomic contexts
Refined and adapted based on Dr. Hao’s guidance and feedback.
Conducted an extensive literature review of over 200 articles on structural and functional MRI
Synthesized research findings and created detailed tables that effectively captured key insights.
Interpersonal Emotion Regulation Lab, Manchester Centre for Health Psychology
Development of Interpersonal Emotion Regulation Across Cultures
Conducted thematic analyses on 2500+ qualitative responses, i
dentified significant between-group differences using Latent Profile Analysis on
Examined age-related differences in emotion regulation strategies
Investigated developmental patterns of interpersonal emotion regulation using longitudinal data
Analyzed how emotion regulation capabilities evolve across different cultural contexts
Project 2: Changing Others’ Emotions using EMAs Assessments
Led the recruitment process, successfully enlisting
individuals for an intensive week-long study utilizing Ecological Momentary Assessments.
Evaluating Language Models in Detecting Emotion Regulation Strategies
Independently designed a comprehensive methodology for training Language Models. Detailed guidelines included in papers’ appendix.
Designed a descriptive flow chart within the manuscript.
Pain and Developmental Outcomes in Pediatric Sickle Cell Disease (SCD)
Project 1: Index of Pain Experience in Sickle Cell Anaemia
Prof. Christina Liossi, University of Southampton
data from a randomised-controlled clinical trial on
Authored the first draft of the manuscript and designed a pain heatmap using Photoshop.
Incorporated feedback from peer reviews to refine and enhance the manuscript.
The Interaction between Pain and Executive Function in Paediatric Patients with SCD
Investigated developmental trajectories of executive function in pediatric SCD patients
Analyzed caregiver reports to understand how chronic pain impacts cognitive development
Studied family dynamics and caregiver-child relationships in managing developmental challenges
PSYC21032 Interventions to Improve Mental Health
Facilitated weekly workshops supporting students in applying psychological intervention principles to real-world clinical scenarios.
Supported students in scale development and psychometric research methods; assessed and provided written feedback on student assignments.
PSYC33022 Psychology in the Real World
Led seminars for final-year undergraduates, fostering critical engagement with applied psychological topics and facilitating evidence-based discussions.
International Society for the Study of Individual Differences, Durham, UK
Leveraged my personal perspective as an Asian female student to shed light on potential biases and challenges faced, offering tangible solutions.
Spearheaded discussions and initiatives focused on improving the representation and inclusivity of diverse voices in academic, social, and policy-making platforms
Latent Profile Analysis and Dynamic Structural Equation Model)
Fluent in English and Mandarin; Familiar with Cantonese.
Division of Human Communication, Development & Hearing
